form.label: Root Signal & Behavior Analysis
form.description: Analyze underlying failure signals and behavioral shifts to understand why errors occur, where they originate, and how system behavior evolves over time.

input.label: Select the index containing log data.

chart.title: Breaks down dominant failure signals by categorizing error messages into human-readable types such as timeout, network, or authentication.

search.query: `ops_base_search($index$)` | where is_error=1 | rex field=raw "(?<error_type>$error_keywords$)" | eval error_type = coalesce(error_type, "other") | stats count AS error_count BY error_type | sort - error_count

table.title: Highlights services experiencing elevated error rates, helping isolate components contributing most to operational risk.

search.query: `ops_base_search($index$)` | stats sum(is_error) AS errors count AS total BY service | eval error_rate = round((errors/total)*100, 2) | sort - error_rate

chart.title: Reveals hosts generating a disproportionate share of failures, indicating potential unhealthy nodes or configuration issues.

search.query: `ops_base_search($index$)` | where is_error=1 | stats count AS error_count BY extracted_host | sort - error_count | head 10

table.title: Correlates services with dominant error types to accelerate root cause identification and triage.

search.query: `ops_base_search($index$)` | where is_error=1 | rex field=raw "(?<error_type>$error_keywords$)" | eval error_type = coalesce(error_type, "other") | stats count AS error_count BY service error_type | sort - error_count | head 20

chart.title: Visualizes shifts in log severity distribution over time (for example, INFO or WARN transitioning into ERROR), indicating changes in system behavior.

search.query: index=$index$ | timechart span=1h count BY severity
